product_brief: An AWS managed service to create and control keys used to encrypt or digitally sign data.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), PQC algorithms, or any plans for PQC migration. It only mentions current standards like ECDSA for signing and symmetric encryption.
crypto_primitives: ECDSA (for signing keys generated by AWS Encryption SDK), Symmetric key algorithms (specific suite not named, but described as "secure, authenticated"), HMAC
key_management_model: Cloud-managed service where keys are created, managed, used, and deleted entirely within AWS KMS; protected by FIPS 140-3 Level 3 validated HSMs; supports customer-managed keys with key policies.
architecture_type: Managed cloud service (SaaS) backed by hardware security modules (HSM); client-side encryption library (AWS Encryption SDK) available for application integration.
license: Apache 2.0 (for AWS Encryption SDK); Commercial/Proprietary (for AWS KMS managed service)
latest_version: Not stated (mentions versions earlier than 1.7.x and versions 2.0.x for the SDK, but no specific latest version number)
fips_validated: Yes, FIPS 140-3 Security Level 3 validated hardware security modules (HSM)
primary_platforms: AWS Cloud; Programming languages: C, .NET, Java, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Go, CLI
signature_schemes: ECDSA (generated by AWS Encryption SDK for signing); Asymmetric key pairs supported in AWS KMS for signing operations.
authoritative_source_url: Not stated (document text contains navigation links but no specific product URL)

product_name: Google Cloud KMS (Cloud Key Management Service)
product_brief: A cloud-hosted key management service to centrally manage symmetric and asymmetric cryptographic keys on Google Cloud.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), quantum-resistant algorithms, or any PQC migration plans. Only classical algorithms are listed.
crypto_primitives: AES256, RSA 2048, RSA 3072, RSA 4096, EC P256, EC P384
key_management_model: Cloud-hosted service supporting Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK), External Key Manager (EKM) for keys stored outside Google infrastructure, and Hardware Security Module (HSM) integration. Supports key rotation, import, and destruction with a 24-hour delay.
architecture_type: Managed cloud service (SaaS) with options for HSM-backed keys and External Key Manager (EKM) integration.
fips_validated: Yes, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated HSMs are supported.
primary_platforms: Google Cloud (including GKE, BigQuery, Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Dataproc, Data Fusion)
target_industries: Financial Services, Healthcare and Life Sciences, Government, Retail, Media and Entertainment, Telecommunications, Manufacturing, Supply Chain and Logistics, Education, Startups and SMB
regulatory_status: Supports compliance mandates requiring hardware encryption (HSM), key separation (EKM), and FIPS 140-2.
signature_schemes: RSA 2048, RSA 3072, RSA 4096, EC P256, EC P384 (used for signing operations with HSM)

product_brief: Industry leading enterprise key management solution enabling centralized encryption key management, access control, and security policy configuration.
pqc_support: No mention of specific PQC implementation or support in the provided text.
pqc_capability_description: The document mentions a "Quantum" section advising enterprises to start planning for post-quantum readiness and offers a risk assessment tool, but does not state that CipherTrust Manager currently implements PQC algorithms or supports PQC migration features.
crypto_primitives: Not stated (Text mentions integration with HSMs but does not list specific algorithms like ECDSA, RSA, etc.)
key_management_model: Centralized management of key lifecycle (generation, rotation, destruction, import/export) using FIPS 140-3 Level 3 compliant Thales Luna or third-party Hardware Security Modules (HSMs).
architecture_type: HSM-backed; available as virtual and physical appliances.
license: Proprietary (implied by "Commercial" context and lack of open source mention)
fips_validated: Yes; integrates with FIPS 140-3 Level 3 compliant Thales Luna or third-party HSMs.
primary_platforms: AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, VMware, Microsoft HyperV (as native virtual machines); On-premises physical and virtualized infrastructures.
target_industries: Financial Services, Government, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Retail, Telecommunications, Automotive, Critical Infrastructure, Education, Insurance Providers, Media and Entertainment, SaaS Providers.
regulatory_status: Supports compliance with FIPS 140-2, FIPS 140-3, GDPR, PCI DSS, HIPAA, SOC 2, SOX, NIST 800-53, FedRamp, and various regional regulations (e.g., LGPD, CCPA, DORA).
pqc_roadmap_details: Not stated (Text only mentions general advice to start planning for post-quantum readiness).
